rootCauseAnalysis.summary: Five PersistentVolumeClaims (batch-output-job-1 through batch-output-job-5) are orphaned in the demo-orphaned-pvc namespace after their batch Job workloads completed and were deleted, leaving 500Mi of local-path storage bound but unconsumed with no automated cleanup mechanism.
contributingFactors: Batch Jobs that created PVCs batch-output-job-1 through batch-output-job-5 have been deleted, leaving PVCs without owning workloads
PVCs carry label 'batch-run=completed' confirming the batch run finished but no cleanup was performed
No TTL or automated cleanup mechanism exists for post-batch PVC lifecycle management — no Jobs, CronJobs, or batch controllers remain in the namespace
All 5 PVCs (100Mi each, 500Mi total) remain in Bound phase on a single node (kubernaut-demo-worker2), wasting local-path storage
The data-processor Deployment is unrelated to the orphaned PVCs — it uses only a ConfigMap volume and is healthy, confirming PVCs have no active consumers
PVC protection finalizer (kubernetes.io/pvc-protection) prevents accidental deletion, so manual or automated cleanup is required

kaResponse.analysis: Five PersistentVolumeClaims (batch-output-job-1 through batch-output-job-5) are orphaned in the demo-orphaned-pvc namespace after their batch Job workloads completed and were deleted, leaving 500Mi of local-path storage bound but unconsumed with no automated cleanup mechanism.
